name: Bryan Kim
title: Assistant Professor
college: College of Performance, Visualization, & Fine Arts
department: Technical Art and Interactive Media — Game Design
bio: Assistant Professor at Texas A&M University researching the intersection of game design, health, and interactive technology. My work focuses on making games that heal, teach, and connect people — from VR digital therapeutics for stroke patients to mobile games that help children with autism build social skills. I believe play is one of the most powerful tools we have for human wellbeing.
funFact: I believe the best games are the ones where you forget you're learning. This website is my proof of concept — you're playing one right now.

experience.title: Founder & CEO
experience.description: Led a health game startup from inception. Secured $210K investment. Built five therapeutic games for visual rehab, ASD support, and children's health education.

experience.title: Producer — Fortnite Battle Royale
experience.description: Shipped Fishing, Harpoon Gun, Swimming, Motorboat, Lightsabers, Star Wars live event, Batarang, Bat Grappler, and Taco Time. Coordinated daily company-wide playtests with 100+ testers.

lab.name: TAMU Game Lab
lab.description: A research lab exploring the intersection of game design, health, and interactive technology. We build games that serve as therapeutic tools, educational platforms, and vehicles for social good.
lab.openCall: Looking for motivated students passionate about using games to improve lives. If you believe play can be medicine, let's talk.

projects.title: Coming Soon
projects.description: New lab projects will be listed here as they launch.

projects.title: Eye Rehab
projects.description: VR digital therapeutics for stroke-related visual deficits.
projects.detail: Incorporated eye-tracking technology in VR to engage patients in therapeutic exercises. Completed IRB-approved clinical testing with two cases.

projects.title: Emotion Adventure
projects.description: Mobile game for ASD children — emotion and social skills training.
projects.detail: Designed to enhance cognitive, social, and emotion-situation recognition for children with ASD. 50+ downloads from families and clinicians.

projects.title: LunchLaunch
projects.description: Mobile educational shooting game for children.
projects.detail: Transformed traditional shooting mechanics into educational experiences for children.

projects.title: HayStackToe
projects.detail: Reimagines Tic-tac-toe as a collaborative cross-platform party game for children.

projects.title: Double Walker
projects.description: Mobile AR self-care game for ASD children.
projects.detail: An augmented reality self-care experience designed for children with Autism Spectrum Disorder.

projects.title: ERL Soil Project
projects.description: Artistic science game on soil, nutrient cycle, and climate change.
projects.detail: Room-scale immersive projection using Houdini simulation and AI procedural generation to teach about carbon sequestration and climate change.

projects.title: Arbnb by Carpet
projects.detail: Semester-long academic project at the ETC exploring augmented reality.

projects.title: Burnt Onion Studio
projects.description: Year-long game dev project modeled on AAA studio practices.
projects.detail: Pre-production package, Scrum, daily stand-ups. A masterclass in team-scale game production.

projects.title: Pupil
projects.description: AR applications for enhancing classroom learning.
projects.detail: Playtested with five schools. International recognition for AR classroom innovation.

projects.title: NEO
projects.description: Online crowd-sourcing PC game for mapping neural networks.
projects.detail: Collaborated with Princeton. Playtested with 200 players to refine the design.

projects.title: HOME
projects.detail: Final round project in the renowned BVW course at CMU's ETC.

projects.title: Dragon Solo Project
projects.description: Building Virtual Worlds Round 0 — solo project.

projects.title: 2019 Pittsburgh Global Game Jam
projects.detail: 48-hour game jam at the Pittsburgh Global Game Jam site.

publications.title: Game Mechanics and Aesthetics Design Framework in Digital Therapeutics for Stroke-related Visual Impairment
publications.authors: Bryan Kim; Ye Chan Kim; Hyun Jin Shin; Jee-in Kim
publications.plainEnglish: We created a reusable blueprint for designing therapeutic games, tested on stroke patients with vision problems.

publications.title: Game-Driven Practices for Social Skills: Exploring Usability for Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder
publications.authors: Bryan Kim; Ye Chan Kim; Jin Joo Son; Da-Yea Song; Ill Jin Lee; Jae Hyun Han; Doori Kim; Hee Jeong Yoo; Jee-In Kim
publications.plainEnglish: Can games teach kids with autism to navigate social situations? We built one and tested it.

publications.title: Using Selective Simulation to Create Digital Therapeutics for Educating Social Skills to Children with High-Functioning ASD
publications.plainEnglish: We gamified specific social moments instead of simulating everything — making therapy more focused and effective.

publications.title: ASDCLAIMS: Twitter Dataset of Claims on Autism Spectrum Disorder
publications.authors: Michelle Kim; Bryan Kim; Jung Hwan Kim; Jee-In Kim
publications.plainEnglish: We analyzed how people talk about autism on social media, building a dataset for misinformation research.

publications.title: Game With A Purpose To Collect Home Audio Data
publications.authors: Bryan Kim; Yi Cheng; Zixuan Li; Ruoxi Li; Chenchen Tan; Shuo Wang; Yifeng Shi; Jessica Hammer
publications.plainEnglish: We turned boring audio data collection into a game people actually wanted to play.

publications.title: MRI-based 3D Simulation of Transtrochanteric Anterior Rotational Osteotomy for Osteonecrosis of Femoral Head
publications.authors: Jun-Young Kim; Jaeyeong Park; Bryan Kim; et al.
publications.plainEnglish: We used 3D modeling from MRI scans to simulate a complex hip surgery — helping surgeons plan better.

awards: Founders Award of Excellence — 29th Annual Honors Convocation (2023)
K-Unicorn Academy — Korea SMEs and Startups Agency (2021)

stageLabels.collaborator: You really get what drives Bryan
